display.purpose: Page H1 / hero title; one per view, one accent word.

body.purpose: Descriptions and prose; secondary copy in Muted.

label.purpose: Mono uppercase eyebrows, field labels, table headers.

mono.purpose: All numbers, units, IDs, table cells; tabular figures. The system signature.

shadows.name: ambient-hero
shadows.purpose: The sync command center hero card only; the one truly floating surface.

shadows.name: accent-glow-action
shadows.purpose: Primary action button inside the sync command center.

shadows.name: preset-active
shadows.purpose: Active range-preset chip in the filter rail.

shadows.name: shadow-soft
shadows.purpose: Referenced via var(--shadow-soft) on the date popover and active toggles, but never declared in any stylesheet, so it currently no-ops. Intended as a quiet ambient lift; define it before relying on it.

motion.name: transition-fast
motion.purpose: Nav items, segmented toggles, show-more, chart bar hover.

motion.name: transition-standard
motion.purpose: Buttons, KPI hover, theme/locale toggles, theme background/color swap.

motion.name: pulse
motion.value: 2s infinite (@keyframes pulse: expanding sage box-shadow ring)
motion.purpose: Live endpoint heartbeat dot and status pill.

motion.name: reduced-motion
motion.purpose: No prefers-reduced-motion alternative exists for pulse or transitions; add one to meet WCAG 2.1 AA.

breakpoints.name: lg
breakpoints.purpose: Sidebar 248px to 220px; hero and grid-2 collapse to one column; KPI grid 4 to 2.

breakpoints.name: sm
breakpoints.purpose: Sidebar becomes static (non-sticky); everything stacks to a single column.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Ink-filled primary action (Sync). Hover deepens to pure black; no shadow.

components.name: Default Button
components.description: Quiet secondary button on white surface; hover darkens the hairline border.

components.name: Local Tag
components.description: Mono pill marking the local-only posture; Terracotta soft fill, deep text.

components.name: KPI Card
components.description: Single metric card; mono tabular value, uppercase label. Hover darkens border.

components.name: Panel
components.description: The workhorse light container; white surface, hairline border, 14px radius.

components.name: Instrument Card
components.description: The signature dark readout surface (trends/status/sync); dark in both themes, depth by contrast.

components.name: Data Bar Row
components.description: Signature distribution row: mono name, Terracotta fill with a deep cap edge, mono tabular value.

narrative.overview: llmusage looks like a precision meter that lives on your machine. The page is warm paper; the data lives on dark, calibrated readout panels set into it; every number is monospace. The split is the whole idea: the warm light surface is the housing you read in, and the dark instrument cards (trends, run status, the sync command center) are the lit display where the measurements actually appear. Depth comes from that contrast, not from drop shadows. The voice is trustworthy, precise, calm: costs are labeled estimates, low-sample sources degrade visibly, and the local-only posture is shown on the surface rather than asserted in copy. It rejects four neighbors: hype-y SaaS dashboards, dense neon-on-black ops cockpits, generic admin templates, and playful consumer-fintech trackers. It is a developer's measuring tool.
keyCharacteristics: Warm paper light theme (#f6f3ee) and warm near-black dark theme (#14120f); full data-theme parity.
A single brand accent, Terracotta (#c8553d). Every other color is neutral or a status semantic.
Monospace carries all data; the sans carries prose and titles. The split is strict.
Dark instrument cards embedded in the light page; depth by contrast, not shadow.
Flat surfaces, 1px hairline borders, restrained radii (8-18px), pills reserved for toggles and tags.
Bilingual (EN / ZH) and theme-switchable, built to stay legible in all four combinations.

rules.name: The Single Voice Rule
rules.body: Terracotta is the only brand color and stays at roughly <=15% of any surface. Sage, Ochre, and Brick are status signals, never styling.

rules.name: The Instrument Rule
rules.body: Data-dense readouts render on the dark instrument family in every theme. Depth is the contrast between warm housing and dark display, not a shadow.

rules.name: The Mono-Data Rule
rules.body: Every number, unit, code-like token, ID, table cell, and label is set in the monospace face with tabular figures. The sans face is for prose and titles only.

rules.name: The One Accent Word Rule
rules.body: The hero title emphasizes exactly one word in Terracotta. Hierarchy otherwise comes from size and weight, not color.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest. Separation is a 1px hairline or a tonal step. Shadow is reserved for the one hero card and overlays.

dos: Do set every number, unit, label, and table cell in the monospace face with tabular figures. The Mono-Data Rule is the system's signature.
Do keep data-dense readouts on the dark instrument family and let warm-vs-dark contrast create the depth.
Do hold Terracotta to <=15% of a surface: eyebrows, bar fills, links, active states, one featured KPI.
Do separate light surfaces with 1px line hairlines and the paper to raised to white tonal steps.
Do keep card radii in the 14-18px band; reserve 999px pills for toggles, tags, and badges.
Do verify body text hits 4.5:1 in both themes. Muted (#8a8278) on Warm Paper is ~3.5:1; nudge secondary body copy toward Ink-2 where it carries real content.
Do define the two referenced-but-missing tokens: --shadow-soft (soft lift for popovers and active toggles) and --danger (the Brick error color high-severity findings expect).
Do add a prefers-reduced-motion: reduce alternative for the endpoint pulse animation and the standard 0.15-0.18s transitions.
donts: Don't build hype-y SaaS dashboards: no gradient hero-metric template, no supercharge-your-workflow copy, no vanity-metric theater.
Don't drift toward a dense neon-on-black ops cockpit (Datadog / Grafana). Every panel must answer a real question.
Don't ship generic admin-template identical card grids (icon + heading + text, repeated).
Don't adopt playful or gamified consumer-fintech treatments. This is a precise developer tool, not a money app.
Don't introduce a second brand hue. Terracotta is the only accent; Sage / Ochre / Brick are status semantics.
Don't set a metric in the sans face, or a sentence in mono.
Don't pair a 1px border with a wide drop shadow (the ghost-card pattern), and don't round any card past 18px.
Don't distinguish chart series by hue alone; the chart ramp is one accent. Use labels, position, or luminance.
Don't add a section eyebrow to a new section by reflex; the uppercase-mono eyebrow is already at saturation.
